pattern_context.pattern_description: Detects external calls before state updates - violates CEI pattern
pattern_context.why_flagged: External call on line 47 (msg.sender.call) occurs BEFORE state update on line 49 (balances[msg.sender] -= amount). This allows an attacker contract to re-enter during the call and drain additional funds.

steps.evidence_needed: External call with value transfer found
State update occurs AFTER external call
steps.red_flag: Balance update on line 49 happens after call on line 47
msg.sender receives the call - attacker controls fallback
steps.safe_if: Call target is a trusted contract

questions_to_answer: Is there an external call before the state update?
Can an attacker re-enter this function during the call?
What is the maximum damage if exploited?

test_context.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker deploys malicious contract with fallback
2. Attacker deposits 1 ETH to get balance
3. Attacker calls withdraw(1 ETH)
4. During the call, attacker's fallback re-enters withdraw
5. Since balance not yet updated, check passes again
6. Repeat until vault drained
test_context.expected_outcome: Attacker drains vault beyond their deposit amount

similar_exploits.name: The DAO Hack
similar_exploits.summary: Classic reentrancy attack that drained 3.6M ETH from The DAO

fix_recommendations: Apply CEI pattern: Update balances[msg.sender] -= amount BEFORE the external call
Add nonReentrant modifier from OpenZeppelin ReentrancyGuard
Consider using pull payment pattern instead of push
